other_observers: Maggie Boswell, Joey Kellner, Kathy Mihm-Dunning, Steve Larson
description: Obvious male quail with a black head plume. Black eye and throat, with a white eyeline and white vertical stripe. White spots on the hindneck and scaled underparts with a chestnut belly patch distinguish this species from Gambel's Quail. White streaking on the sides with a dark gray back; dark yellowish legs: see attached photographs.  The bird called quite frequently, and sang once on the morning of April 14.
